untranslatable_reason: Pattern identification criteria for 'Bump-and-Run Reversal Bottoms' are not fully specified in the source material. The signal requires precise definition of: (1) how to identify the initial downtrend/bump, (2) the specific geometry of the two trend lines that form the pattern, (3) exact breakout confirmation criteria above the upper trend line. Without these technical specifications, the pattern cannot be reliably encoded using available indicators. The Donchian channel approximation above is a reasonable proxy for trend line breakout but does not capture the full pattern complexity.
translation_notes: Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) as a proxy for trend line breakouts, but this is approximate. Recommended: obtain detailed pattern definition from Bulkowski's work including exact measurements of bump depth, trend line slopes, and breakout volume confirmation. Historical performance data shows 98-99% success rate on 5% rises and 38% average rise in bull markets with 186 days to ultimate high.
